articles.title: A Democratic Electrician Nabs a State Senate Seat in Republican Florida
articles.summary: With Brian Nathan’s victory certified, Democrats won two of three state legislative races in this month’s special elections, all in Republican-leaning districts.

articles.title: Israel Passes Law to Hang Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
articles.summary: Experts say the law was written in such a way as to ensure that it was unlikely to ever apply to Jewish extremists who commit similar crimes.

articles.title: Who’s ‘Safer’ or ‘Riskier’? Maine Democrats Clash Over How to Beat Collins.
articles.summary: Gov. Janet Mills argues that her rival for Senate, Graham Platner, could be doomed by his history of offensive online remarks. But at a time of anti-establishment anger, Mr. Platner says he is the saf

articles.title: Michael Rousseau, Air Canada’s CEO, to Step Down Amid Backlash Over Comments After Crash
articles.summary: Michael Rousseau’s mostly English statement after a fatal runway collision drew sharp criticism. The airline said it would emphasize French fluency in its search for a successor.

articles.title: T.S.A. Lines Appear to Be Getting Shorter as Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks
articles.summary: The hourslong waits at airports appear to be easing after the president signed an order to pay Transportation Security Administration officers.

articles.title: Trump Claims ‘Regime Change’ in Iran Is Already Complete
articles.summary: President Trump suggested that Iran’s current leaders were “much more reasonable” as he sought to show progress in his war aims. Iran’s power structure appears firmly in control despite a month of U.S

articles.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
articles.summary: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

articles.title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
articles.summary: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.

articles.title: NASA is just days away from historic Artemis II moon launch
articles.summary: On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.

articles.title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
articles.summary: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.

articles.title: Who is an American? The Supreme Court will decide
articles.summary: President Trump claims that there is no automatic guarantee to birthright citizenship in the Constitution. But, will that claim hold up in court?

articles.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
articles.summary: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump is weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
articles.summary: President Trump said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

articles.title: Philanthropy is a 'significant form of power.' Here's how Jeffrey Epstein exploited that
articles.summary: A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.

articles.title: Trump administration cuts turned rural towns into sitting ducks for disasters
articles.summary: The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.

articles.title: As birthright citizenship goes to Supreme Court, here's how Americans feel about it
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear arguments Wednesday on whether all children born in the United States can continue to automatically receive citizenship.

articles.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
articles.summary: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

articles.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
articles.summary: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

articles.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
articles.summary: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

articles.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
articles.summary: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
